title: Tarenna subsessilis
intro: Tarenna subsessilis, also known as the 'Bengal Tarenna', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. Tarenna subsessilis has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine and has shown potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Tarenna subsessilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Tarenna subsessilis is often prepared as a decoction, a tea, or a powder, and is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Tarenna subsessilis is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes. Tarenna subsessilis belongs to the tribe Gardenieae. Within this tribe, the plant is placed in the genus Tarenna, which includes various species of flowering plants found in tropical regions. The classification of Tarenna subsessilis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Gentianales, Family: Rubiaceae, Tribe: Gardenieae, Genus: Tarenna.
uses: Tarenna subsessilis is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant's extracts are also used to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Additionally, Tarenna subsessilis is used as an antimalarial agent due to its potential to inhibit the growth of malaria-causing parasites. Further research is needed to fully explore its medicinal applications and potential benefits.
